title: When millions of AI agents meet
summary: Nenad Tomašev discusses the emergence of autonomous AI agents that can execute multi-step plans and the theoretical framework of an "agentic economy" where millions of agents transact and negotiate with each other. The conversation covers operational shifts to cooperative systems of specialists, psychological risks from automation bias, and cybersecurity challenges including dynamic cloaking and agentic traps for securing distributed intelligence.
writeup: Nenad Tomašev of Google DeepMind frames a shift from single AI agents that execute multi-step plans toward an "agentic economy," where millions of agents transact and negotiate with one another. The discussion treats this as a move to cooperative systems of specialists rather than monolithic tools, and it weighs the operational changes that follow from delegating work to autonomous agents. It names concrete risks: automation bias as a psychological hazard, and cybersecurity threats including dynamic cloaking and agentic traps. The takeaway is that securing distributed intelligence is a central problem once agents act and coordinate at scale.
